Q9 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A sum of Rs 90,000 is to be distributed amongst NOT more than 18 students as prize amount in denomination of Rs 500, Rs 2500, Rs 12,500 and Rs 62,500 such that there is at least one student getting a prize of each denomination and each student gets exactly one prize. The entire amount has to be distributed. In how many ways this amount can be distributed?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(b)
We will start with one student each being recipient of one prize value. This implies that
62500 + 12500 + 2500 + 500 = Rs 78000 has been used up leaving a sum of Rs 12,000 to be distributed
among NOT more than 18 – 4 = 14 students. Since the amount remaining is less than Rs 12,500, this
amount has to be distributed as prizes of denomination Rs 2,500 and/or Rs 500 only.
We can start with four students receiving Rs 500 each and the remaining Rs 10,000 can be given to
four more students @ Rs 2,500 leading to total number of 12 students receiving the prizes.
Alternately, we can have 4 + 5 = 9 students receiving Rs 500 each and the remaining Rs 7,500 can
be given to three more students @ Rs 2,500 leading to total number of 16 students receiving the
prizes.
Any more options are ruled out since we have to increase in steps of 5 – 1 = 4 students only which
means that the next option will lead to 20 students getting the prize money which is not possible
since there are only 18 students in all.

Q17 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following techniques reduce the error due to overfitting on training data?

Correct answer: A, B
Solution

(a, b)
• Regularization adds a penalty to large weights, forcing the model to stay simpler. This prevents
the model from memorizing noise in the training data. As a result, the model generalizes
better to unseen samples. So, option (a) is correct.
• Cross-validation evaluates the model on multiple validation sets. It helps detect when the
model performs well only on training data but poorly elsewhere. Thus, it guides the selection
of a model that generalizes properly. So, option (b) is correct.
• Making the model deeper or adding more parameters increases its capacity to memorize
training data. This leads to capturing noise rather than meaningful patterns. Therefore,
complexity generally increases overfitting. So, option (c) is incorrect.
• Training accuracy is not a reliable indicator of real performance. A model with high training
accuracy may still fail on unseen data. Depending only on it usually results in stronger
overfitting. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q19 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1 : Beam search prunes nodes outside the beam at each level.
S2 : Beam search is a complete search algorithm.
S3 : Beam search with beam width = 1 behaves like Hill Climbing.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Beam search keeps only a fixed number of top nodes (beam width) at each level.
All other nodes are discarded, which helps reduce memory and computation. So, S1 is correct.
• Since nodes outside the beam are discarded, some paths may never be explored. Therefore, it may fail to find a solution, so it is not complete. So, S2 is incorrect.
• With beam width = 1, only the best node is selected at each step.
This is exactly the behavior of greedy hill climbing, which always chooses the best immediate option. So, S3 is correct.

Q20 NAT Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Consider the following Python code:

 
What will the output of the above given code?
NAT
Correct answer: 10
Solution

10
1. j = –3, –2, –1: j >= 0 is False → skip → count = 0, i = 0.
2. j = 0: j >= 0 True, i = 0 → condition False, i += 1 → i = 1, count = 0.
3. j = 1: condition True (i = 1) → count = 0 + 1 = 1, i = 2.
4. j = 2: count = 1 + 2 = 3, i = 3.
5. j = 3: count = 3 + 3 = 6, i = 4.
After loop: count += i → 6 + 4 = 10
Output: 10

Q30 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements are correct?

Correct answer: A, B, D
Solution

(a, b, d)
• Roll-up aggregates data by moving from a lower level (detailed) to a higher level (summarized)
in a dimension hierarchy. Hence, the level of detail decreases and data granularity is reduced.
So, option (a) correct.
• Drill-down moves from summarized data to finer levels of detail. This exposes more detailed
information, thereby increasing data granularity. So, option (b) correct.
• Roll-up and drill-down are navigation/query operations performed on the data cube. They
do not change or modify the stored data in the data warehouse. So, option (c) incorrect.
• Drill-down only displays existing detailed data that was previously aggregated. No new
data values are created; only the level of detail is changed. So, option (d) correct.

Q38 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following methods for dimensionality reduction belong to the category of feature selection rather than feature extraction?

Correct answer: A, C
Solution

(a, c)
• This method eliminates original features based on a statistical criterion. No new features are
created; hence it is a feature selection technique. So, option (a) is correct.
• LDA projects data onto a new lower-dimensional space by forming linear combinations of
original features. Since new features are constructed, it is feature extraction. So, option (b) is incorrect.
• Highly correlated features are removed to reduce redundancy while keeping the original
feature set unchanged. Therefore, it belongs to feature selection. So, option (c) is correct.
• PCA creates new orthogonal components by combining original features. This transformation
makes PCA a feature extraction method. So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q53 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements are correct?

Correct answer: B, C, D
Solution

(b, c, d)
• MLP hidden layers cannot use a threshold (step) function because it is not differentiable, and
gradient-based learning (backpropagation) would fail. So, option (a) is incorrect.
• MLPs use non-linear, differentiable activation functions like ReLU, sigmoid, or tanh in hidden
layers. So, option (b) is correct.
• MLPs can solve non-linear problems, unlike a single-layer perceptron which can only handle
linearly separable data. So, option (c) is correct.
• Backpropagation is the standard algorithm used to update weights in a multilayer perceptron.
So, option (d) is correct.

Q54 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

K-Means clustering is highly sensitive to the initial placement of centroids. Which of the following methods can reduce this sensitivity?

Correct answer: A, B
Solution

(a, b)
• Selecting the clustering with the lowest sum of squared distances after multiple runs reduces
the impact of poor initial centroids. So, option (a) is correct.
• K-Means++ chooses initial centroids that are far apart, improving convergence and stability.
So, option (b) is correct.
• DBSCAN is a different clustering method; it does not address K-Means' initialization problem.
So, option (c) is incorrect.
• Scaling centroids does not solve sensitivity to initial placement; it only changes magnitude.
So, option (d) is incorrect.

Q55 MSQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Which statements are correct regarding Bayesian network inference algorithms?

Correct answer: B, C
Solution

(b, c)
• Exact inference can be exponentially complex in networks with many variables or loops,
because intermediate factors in algorithms like variable elimination can grow very large. So, option (a) is incorrect.
• It computes exact conditional probabilities by summing out hidden variables, but the size of
intermediate factors can become exponential in densely connected networks. So, option (b) is
correct.
• Methods like Gibbs sampling, rejection sampling, and likelihood weighting generate finite
samples, so they provide approximate probabilities, converging to exact results only
asymptotically. So, option (c) is correct.
• Belief propagation is exact only for tree-structured networks; in networks with loops, it
becomes loopy belief propagation, which is approximate and may not converge. So,
option (d) is incorrect.

Q64 MCQ Not checked
Technical • Data Science and artificial Intelligence • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following is correct about the Hill Climbing search algorithm?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Hill Climbing is a greedy local search that moves to better neighbors. It is memory-efficient but
can get stuck in local maxima, ridges, or plateaus, so it does not guarantee the global optimum.
